options.name: --no-allow-insecure-repositories
options.description: Forbid the update command to acquire unverifiable data from configured sources

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Install the newest versions of all packages currently installed on the system

options.name: --with-new-pkgs
options.description: Allow installing new packages when used in conjunction with upgrade

subcommands.name: dist-upgrade
subcommands.description: Same as upgrade, but also handles changing dependencies with new versions of packages

subcommands.name: dselect-upgrade
subcommands.description: Follows the changes made by dselect(1) to the Status field of available packages

options.name: --no-upgrade
options.description: Do not upgrade packages

options.name: --only-upgrade
options.description: Do not install new packages

name: --auto-remove
--autoremove
options.description: Acts like running the autoremove command

name: --auto-remove
--autoremove
options.description: Acts like running the autoremove command

subcommands.name: purge
subcommands.description: Same as remove, but also deletes configuration files

options.name: --compile
options.description: The package will be compiled to a binary .deb

options.name: --download-only
options.description: The source package will not be unpacked

name: -a
--host-architecture
options.description: Controls the architecture packages are built for by apt-get source --compile and how cross-builddependencies are satisfied

name: -P
--build-profiles
options.description: Controls the activated build profiles for which a source package is built by apt-get source --compile and how build dependencies are satisfied

options.name: --only-source
options.description: Indicates that the given source names are not to be mapped through the binary table

subcommands.name: build-dep
subcommands.description: Install/remove packages in an attempt to satisfy the build dependencies for a source package

options.name: --only-source
options.description: Indicates that the given source names are not to be mapped through the binary table

subcommands.name: check
subcommands.description: Updates the package cache and checks for broken dependencies

subcommands.name: download
subcommands.description: Download the given binary package into the current directory

subcommands.name: clean
subcommands.description: Clears out the local repository of retrieved package files

subcommands.name: autoclean
subcommands.description: Same as clean, but only removes package files that can no longer be downloaded

subcommands.name: autoremove
subcommands.description: Remove packages that were automatically installed to satisfy dependencies for other packages and are no longer needed

subcommands.name: changelog
subcommands.description: Displays the changelog of a package

subcommands.name: indextargets
subcommands.description: Displays a listing of information about all data files (aka index targets) apt-get update would download

options.name: --no-install-recommends
options.description: Do not consider recommended packages as a dependency for installing

options.name: --install-suggests
options.description: Consider suggested packages as a dependency for installing

name: -d
--download-only
options.description: Package files are only retrieved, not unpacked or installed

name: -f
--fix-broken
options.description: Attempt to correct a system with broken dependencies in place

name: -m
--ignore-missing
--fix-missing
options.description: Ignore missing packages

options.name: --no-download
options.description: Disables downloading of packages

name: -q
--quiet
options.description: Produces output suitable for logging, omitting progress indicators

name: -s
--simulate
--just-print
--dry-run
--recon
--no-act
options.description: Perform a simulation of events that would occur based on the current system state but do not actually change the system

name: -y
--yes
--assume-yes
options.description: Automatic yes to all prompts

options.name: --assume-no
options.description: Automatic no to all prompts

name: -u
--show-upgraded
options.description: Show upgraded packages

name: -V
--verbose-versions
options.description: Show full versions for upgraded and installed packages

name: -b
--compile
--build
options.description: Compile source packages after downloading them

options.name: --ignore-hold
options.description: Ignore package holds

options.name: --allow-downgrades
options.description: Dangerous option that will cause apt to continue without prompting if it is doing downgrades

options.name: --allow-remove-essential
options.description: Dangerous option that will cause apt to continue without prompting if it is removing essentials

options.name: -allow-change-held-packages
options.description: Dangerous option that will cause apt to continue without prompting if it is changing held packages

options.name: --force-yes
options.description: Dangerous option that will cause apt to continue without prompting if it is doing something potentially harmful

options.name: --print-uris
options.description: Instead of fetching the files to install their URIs are printed

options.name: --purge
options.description: Use purge instead of remove for anything that would be removed

options.name: --reinstall
options.description: Re-install packages that are already installed and at the newest version

options.name: --list-cleanup
options.description: apt-get will automatically manage the contents of /var/lib/apt/lists to ensure that obsolete files are erased

name: -t
--target-release
--default-release
options.description: controls the default input to the policy engine

options.name: --trivial-only
options.description: Only perform operations that are 'trivial'

options.name: --no-remove
options.description: If any packages are to be removed immediately aborts without prompting

name: --diff-only
--dsc-only
--tar-only
options.description: Download only the diff, dsc, or tar file of a source archive

options.name: --arch-only
options.description: Only process architecture-dependent build-dependencies

options.name: --allow-unauthenticated
options.description: Ignore if packages can't be authenticated and don't prompt about it

options.name: --show-progress
options.description: Show user friendly progress information in the terminal window when packages are installed, upgraded or removed

name: -h
--help
options.description: Show a short usage summary

name: -v
--version
options.description: Show the program version

name: -c
--config-file
options.description: Specify a configuration file to use

name: -o
--option
options.description: Set a configuration option
